page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor operating at 3.9 GHz, configured with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The memory subsystem consists of 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and memory-bound execution capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core performance with a score of 2246.0 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.94 GB/sec. Text processing also registers in the top tier. For workloads leveraging advanced instruction sets, the CPU supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This hardware acceleration enables top-tier text generation speeds of 351.82 tokens per second for small 135M parameter language models, though larger 7B and 70B models operate within average performance tiers.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.4xlarge offers a high-frequency CPU paired with a dense DDR5 memory configuration, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications that also require fast sequential processing. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy workloads. This instance is configured for workloads such as database operations, memory-cached applications, real-time data processing, and single-threaded compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processor. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2246.0 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression of 14.94 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier text generation speeds of 351.82 tokens per second for small language models. Lacking local storage, this instance relies on network-attached storage, making it best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-frequency single-threaded computing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, and 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized server combining high-frequency processing with DDR5 memory for demanding database and single-threaded workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
